sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: EIM - EV Municipal Bond Latest Stock News & Market Updates
feed.summary: This article provides news and market updates for EV Municipal Bond (EIM), a non-diversified closed-end municipal bond fund. It covers recent corporate actions, governance updates, and shareholder-related events, including tender offers, annual meeting results, and liquidation proposals supported by major shareholders like Saba Capital. The updates offer insights into the fund's management, shareholder interactions, and potential impacts on liquidity and NAV discounts.

feed.title: Short Interest in Dynex Capital, Inc. (NYSE:DX) Increases By 19.1%
feed.summary: Dynex Capital, Inc. (NYSE:DX) experienced a 19.1% increase in short interest in February, reaching 9,436,132 shares, which represents 5.5% of its stock. The real estate investment trust reported a quarterly EPS miss but announced a monthly dividend yielding 14.9%. Analysts currently rate DX with a "Moderate Buy" and a consensus price target of $14.33.

feed.title: Duke Energy is betting new battery-storage facilities will help it use more renewable energy
feed.summary: Duke Energy has opened a 100-megawatt battery storage facility in Knightdale, NC, which is nearly half of its total battery storage in the state so far. The company is relying on these facilities to help meet its state-mandated goal of being carbon neutral by 2050 by making better use of renewable energy sources like solar. Duke Energy plans to expand its battery storage capabilities significantly, aiming for 5 gigawatts by 2034, which would require building approximately 56 more facilities of similar scale.

feed.title: Is Invitation Homes Inc. (INVH) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Invitation Homes Inc. (INVH), highlighting its position as the largest single-family home leasing company in the U.S. The company benefits from economies of scale, high occupancy rates (97%), and strategic concentration in high-growth markets. INVH also offers an attractive dividend yield of 4.4%, although the author notes a preference for AI stocks with higher upside potential.

feed.title: EnQuest (LON:ENQ) Upgraded to "Neutral" at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has upgraded EnQuest (LON:ENQ) to a "neutral" rating from its previous "underweight," also raising its price target to GBX 25, indicating a potential upside of 43.64%. Despite a negative P/E ratio and high debt-to-equity, the stock shows market consensus for a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of GBX 22.40 from various analysts. EnQuest, an independent energy company focused on optimizing late-life assets and transitioning to renewable energy, saw increased trading volume and has a market capitalization of £323.57 million.

feed.title: Piper Sandler Raises Occidental Petroleum (OXY) Price Target to $54 | OXY Stock News
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has increased its price target for Occidental Petroleum (OXY) to $54 from $47, maintaining a "Neutral" rating. This adjustment comes amidst several other analysts also raising their price targets for OXY in late February 2026. Despite the raised price target, the average analyst target implies a slight downside from its current price, and GuruFocus estimates suggest a larger downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: John Deere expands self-repair tools for farmers
feed.summary: John Deere has empowered farmers with advanced self-repair capabilities through new digital tools. These tools, including the Equipment Mobile app, the Operations Center, and the Pro Service platform, provide farmers with access to maintenance information, diagnostic codes, and step-by-step repair guides. The initiative aims to help farmers maintain and repair their equipment efficiently, even offline, ensuring minimal downtime.

feed.title: Devon Energy (DVN) Price Target Raised by Piper Sandler to $59 | DVN Stock News
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has raised its price target for Devon Energy (DVN) from $55 to $59, maintaining an Overweight rating. This adjustment reflects a 7.27% increase and follows similar positive revisions from other analysts like Barclays, Susquehanna, and Wells Fargo, although Scotiabank lowered its target. Devon Energy, a major oil and gas producer primarily in the Permian Basin, has a consensus "Outperform" status from brokerage firms, with an average target price of $48.87.

feed.title: Colombia's Ecopetrol may lift capex, output amid oil rally
feed.summary: Colombia's state-run oil company Ecopetrol is considering increasing its capital expenditure and production in response to rising global oil prices, which have spiked due to Middle East conflicts. CEO Ricardo Roa stated the company might adjust its capex to the higher end of its guidance if investment potential proves favorable. Additionally, Ecopetrol is exploring cooperation with Venezuela within US sanctions guidelines for oil, gas, and electricity, and plans to invest heavily in its offshore Sirius gas project.

feed.title: Booking (NASDAQ:BKNG) Shares Gap Up - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:BKNG) shares experienced a significant gap up in trading on Thursday, opening at $4,511.10 and reaching $4,540.32 after reporting quarterly earnings that beat analyst expectations. Despite positive earnings, the stock has seen volatility due to broader economic concerns affecting travel demand and several analysts lowering their price targets. Investors are now questioning if the stock is overvalued given its recent climb and upcoming 25-1 stock split.

feed.title: Why Are Salesforce Shares Surging On Thursday?
feed.summary: Salesforce shares climbed on Thursday despite a broader market dip, driven by a strong fourth-quarter performance that exceeded analyst expectations. The company also announced a significant $50 billion share repurchase program and a raised quarterly dividend, alongside a new AI-powered fan companion agent developed with Formula 1. Analysts maintain a bullish outlook on CRM, with several reiterating positive ratings and some trimming price targets.

feed.title: Why Equinox Gold (EQX) Might be Well Poised for a Surge
feed.summary: Equinox Gold (EQX) is showing a strong earnings outlook, with analysts raising estimates for both the current quarter and the full year. This positive trend in revisions has earned the company a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), suggesting potential for continued stock price momentum. Investors might consider adding EQX to their portfolios to capitalize on its earnings growth prospects.

feed.title: Target opens 30 stores this year, including 'food-forward' prototype
feed.summary: Target plans to open 30 new stores this year, reaching a total of 2,000 locations. This expansion is part of a $5 billion investment aimed at reversing recent quarterly sales declines. One of the new store formats will include a "food-forward" prototype.

feed.title: Enphase Speicher: The Solar Battery Upgrade US Homes Crave
feed.summary: The article reviews Enphase Speicher (marketed as IQ Batteries in the US), highlighting its seamless integration with existing Enphase solar systems, intuitive app control, and strong US support infrastructure. While praising its performance for home energy storage and backup, it also discusses premium pricing, potential installation variability, and minor software quirks, advising potential buyers to weigh these factors and obtain multiple quotes.

feed.title: Does Targa Resources' 25% Dividend Hike Outlook Make It A Smart Hold?
feed.summary: Targa Resources (TRGP) has gained significant investor interest due to strong fourth-quarter results, an enhanced Permian growth outlook, and a projected 25% common dividend hike for 2026. The company benefits from robust Permian Basin demand and a substantial pipeline of growth projects, leading to increasing cash flows. Despite these positives, factors like elevated capital spending, reliance on Permian production, and exposure to regional gas price volatility suggest a cautious "Hold" approach for investors.

feed.title: Halliburton’s Oil-Service Play: What US Investors Are Missing
feed.summary: Halliburton is transforming its oil-service business by integrating AI and fracking technology, securing lucrative deals in the Middle East, and focusing on margins rather than volume. The company is positioned as a key infrastructure provider for American energy security and global drilling, benefiting from "higher-for-longer" oil prices and disciplined US shale production. While still a cyclical play tied to fossil fuels, Halliburton offers high-torque exposure to the energy market with increasing tech sophistication for investors comfortable with its inherent volatility.

feed.title: Walmart to add digital shelf labels to all US stores. What to know in Wisconsin
feed.summary: Walmart plans to implement digital shelf labels in all U.S. stores in 2026, building on a rollout that began in 2024. These new digital price tags aim to ensure accurate pricing and save employees time by eliminating manual price changes. Wisconsin, with its 97 Walmart stores, is among the states where shoppers will see this change, which does not involve microphones or cameras.

feed.title: AMETEK Inc. stock: Is this quiet US tech giant your next sleeper win?
feed.summary: AMETEK Inc. is positioned as a quiet but significant player in industrial technology, manufacturing high-precision instruments and electromechanical devices used across various sectors like aerospace, EVs, and medical devices. The article suggests it might be an attractive "sleeper win" for investors seeking long-term growth from a US-centric company with consistent earnings, a robust acquisition strategy, and a focus on essential industrial applications rather than consumer hype. While experts note its strong margins and diversified markets, they also caution about potential valuation risks and cyclical exposure.

feed.title: After-hours volume spike: EQQX.F Invesco NASDAQ-100 (XETRA) 05 Mar 2026, €65.46 liquidity signal
feed.summary: An after-hours volume spike for EQQX.F Invesco NASDAQ-100 on XETRA indicated a liquidity event, with volume surging to 803.00 shares against an average of 6.00 shares, suggesting institutional rebalancing. The ETF closed at €65.46, down 0.91%, with technicals neutral to mildly bearish, and Meyka AI rating it a "HOLD" with a 12-month target of €78.59. Traders are advised caution due to potential volatility around upcoming tech earnings and the ETF's swap-based structure.

feed.title: California's Progressive Energy Policies Support Edison's Growth Investments
feed.summary: The article discusses how California's progressive energy policies are expected to support Edison International's growth initiatives, positioning it favorably among utilities. However, it also highlights the legal and regulatory uncertainties stemming from the Eaton wildfire in January 2025 that involved Edison's equipment, which could impact the company for years. The report, authored by Travis Miller, also notes an extended growth outlook for Edison's core business.

feed.title: Positioning Portfolios for the Energy Transition
feed.summary: This research paper by MSCI analyzes over 37,000 mutual funds and ETFs to determine the link between energy transition characteristics and financial performance. The findings indicate that higher fund Energy Transition Scores were associated with stronger returns between 2022 and 2025, especially for climate-focused funds. The paper introduces a quadrant framework based on MSCI's Energy Transition Framework to help investors position portfolios by assessing transition pressure and readiness, aiding in informed portfolio construction and manager selection.

feed.title: Warren Buffett's Final Top 10 Stock Holdings: Which Ones Will Greg Abel Likely Sell?
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO, Greg Abel, is expected to review Warren Buffett's top 10 holdings. Abel has expressed disappointment with Kraft Heinz and may sell some or all of this stake. Other stocks like Apple, American Express, Moody's, and Coca-Cola are likely to remain foundational, while Occidental Petroleum, Bank of America, Chevron, Chubb, and Alphabet face potential scrutiny.

feed.title: Kroger is closing Little Clinic healthcare locations in Indiana
feed.summary: Kroger is closing several of its in-store Little Clinic healthcare locations in Indiana, with some already shut down and others pending closure. These closures are part of a larger company-wide move, affecting locations in other states like Georgia and Ohio, and come amidst anticipated announcements regarding overall Kroger grocery store closures. Despite the physical clinic shutdowns, Kroger states that nearby locations will remain open and virtual appointments will be available for patients.

feed.title: Expert Outlook: NiSource Through The Eyes Of 4 Analysts
feed.summary: Four analysts have recently provided ratings for NiSource (NYSE: NI), with an average price target of $48.5, marking a 6.01% increase from the previous average. NiSource, a natural gas distribution and electric utility company, demonstrates strong financial performance with positive revenue growth, high net margin, and excellent return on equity and assets, alongside balanced debt management. The article details individual analyst actions, including raised ratings and price target adjustments, offering insights into the company's market standing.

feed.title: Smart Money Is Betting Big In FTNT Options
feed.summary: Benzinga's options scanner detected over 16 option transactions on Fortinet (NASDAQ: FTNT) with a cumulative value of $540,480, alongside 14 puts worth $533,692. Major market movers are focusing on a price band between $80.0 and $86.0 for Fortinet over the last three months. The stock is currently priced at $81.66, up 1.23%, with upcoming earnings expected in 62 days, and analysts maintaining an average price target of $88.8.

feed.title: Vistra's Options: A Look at What the Big Money is Thinking
feed.summary: Large investors are showing a mixed sentiment towards Vistra (NYSE: VST) through unusual options trades, with a slight lean towards bearishness. Benzinga’s scanner identified 13 uncommon options trades, with 2 being puts and 11 calls, signaling significant activity around the $150.0 to $200.0 price range. Despite this, Vistra's stock is currently up, trading at $165.14, and analysts have an average target price of $218.5 for the company.

feed.title: Is DaVita Inc. (DVA) A Good Stock To Buy?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the investment thesis for DaVita Inc. (DVA), a kidney dialysis service provider. While the company maintains strong financial health and generates significant free cash flow, its core challenge is declining patient volumes, which pressures revenue and profitability. The long-term performance hinges on the success of clinical initiatives in reversing this trend, with share buybacks currently driving most EPS growth.

feed.title: DE Gains 33% in a Year: What's the Right Strategy for Investors Now?
feed.summary: Deere & Company (DE) shares have outperformed the market, gaining 33.1% in a year, leading investors to consider its prospects. Despite near-term challenges from low crop prices and tariff impacts on Q1 earnings, DE's long-term growth remains robust due to innovation, geographic expansion, and acquisitions in advanced technologies. However, the stock currently trades at a premium valuation compared to its industry peers and its own historical median, suggesting new investors might want to wait for a better entry point.

feed.title: AU Surges 279.8% in a Year: Should You Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
feed.summary: AngloGold Ashanti's (AU) stock has seen a robust 279.8% surge over the past year, significantly outperforming its industry and peers due to solid FY25 results, increased free cash flow, strategic acquisitions, and rising gold prices. Despite facing higher operating costs, the company's valuation remains attractive, making it a hold for existing investors due to long-term growth prospects, while new investors should note the cost pressures. The company has a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating.

feed.title: Distribution Solutions (DSGR) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Distribution Solutions (DSGR) stock fell 25.3% after reporting Q4 2025 results that missed Wall Street's revenue and profit expectations. The company's revenue was flat year-over-year, and adjusted earnings per share were significantly below estimates, largely due to a sharp decline in operating margin. This significant market reaction indicates a major impact on the perception of the business, with the stock now trading 31.5% below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Why Is Delta Air Lines Stock Falling Thursday?
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) shares fell on Thursday due to increasing investor concerns over surging aviation fuel prices, exacerbated by escalating conflict in the Middle East. This comes amidst a significant executive reshuffle, with new appointments made to key leadership roles. Despite these challenges, the stock carries a "Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $78.40, and Benzinga Edge indicates a mixed but leaning positive outlook with strong value scores.

feed.title: Fidelity National Information Services Refocuses With New Bonds And SaaS Win
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) recently refinanced debt from acquisitions by issuing new bonds in USD and Euro, while also securing a new SaaS client, Integrity Viking Funds, for its Investment Accounting Manager solution. These moves indicate FIS's strategy to reshape its balance sheet and focus on recurring, cloud-based fintech services. Despite declining long-term returns, the company aims to enhance its financial flexibility and expand its role in asset management workflows, as it is also assessed as trading below one estimate of fair value with strong forecast earnings growth.

feed.title: Quince Antitrust Row Over Ugg IP Suits Must Navigate Free Speech
feed.summary: Quince is suing Ugg owner Deckers Outdoor Corp. for alleged antitrust violations, claiming the company filed "sham" trademark lawsuits to monopolize the sheepskin footwear market. The case presents a constitutional challenge, as Quince must prove antitrust violations while navigating the First Amendment protections of companies' rights to litigate. The legal battle highlights the tension between intellectual property enforcement and competitive practices in the billion-dollar slipper market.

feed.title: 5 Energy Stocks Leading This Year's Biggest Rally
feed.summary: The energy sector is seeing a significant rally in 2026, with the Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF up over 20% YTD, driven by geopolitical events, AI infrastructure demand, and mean reversion. This article highlights five energy stocks—Cheniere Energy, Diamondback Energy, ExxonMobil, The Williams Companies, and EOG Resources—that are leading this rally, detailing their recent performance, financials, and future growth prospects. These companies span various oil and gas industries, offering diversified investment opportunities within the surging sector.

feed.title: DFEV Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:DFEV
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information about the Dimensional Emerging Markets Value ETF (DFEV), an actively managed fund aiming for long-term capital appreciation in emerging markets. It outlines key statistics like AUM, dividend yield, expense ratio, and holdings, along with technical and seasonal analyses. The fund invests in a diversified portfolio of value stocks across various sectors and countries, with the advisor having discretion to adjust security exposure.

feed.title: Disabled banker sues Wells Fargo, alleges diversity push drove his firing
feed.summary: A former Wells Fargo Private Mortgage Banker has filed a federal lawsuit against the company, alleging he was fired due to his race, gender, and disability during a diversity-driven restructuring. Bruce Arden claims his position was eliminated because of a company-wide shift towards serving Black, Hispanic, and underserved communities, and that Wells Fargo reclassified his departure as voluntary retirement to deny him severance after his medical leave. The case highlights the increasing challenge for organizations to balance diversity objectives with legal obligations against discrimination.

feed.title: Carnival Corporation: A Low-Risk, Dividend-Yielding 'Buy' For Income Investors (NYSE:CCL)
feed.summary: Carnival Corporation is recommended as a 'buy' for income-focused investors looking for stable, market-aligned returns due to its dividend reinstatement and proprietary island expansion. The company reported record Q4 2025 revenue and an increased net margin, with future growth expected from upcoming upgrades and the unwinding of its dual listing. This move is anticipated to boost index fund demand and share price performance.

feed.title: Technical Analysis of CID HoldCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:DAIC)
feed.summary: This article provides a technical analysis of CID HoldCo, Inc. (NASDAQ: DAIC) on TradingView, presenting a summary of indicators including moving averages, oscillators, and pivots. It indicates a "Sell" signal for various timeframes based on these technical ratings. The article also includes a FAQ section explaining technical ratings, pivots, oscillators, and the reliability of technical analysis as an investment tool.

feed.title: DRVN Investigation Alert: BFA Law Investigates Driven Brands after Financial Restatements Lead to Over 30% Stock Drop
feed.summary: BFA Law is investigating Driven Brands Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:DRVN) after the company announced it would restate financial statements for 2023-2025 due to material accounting errors and identified weaknesses in its internal controls. This news led to a stock drop of over 30% on February 25, 2026. The law firm is looking into potential violations of federal securities laws and encourages affected investors to contact them.

feed.title: SLB on track to record seventh straight session of decline (SLB:NYSE)
feed.summary: SLB N.V. (SLB) is experiencing its seventh consecutive session of decline, with the stock down 1.73% to $47.06 in midday trading. This follows a nearly 7% drop between February 26 and March 4, including a 5.25% fall on March 3. The continued downward trend is putting pressure on the company's stock performance.

feed.title: O’Reilly Auto Parts to pay $5.6M for employee discrimination
feed.summary: O’Reilly Auto Enterprises will pay $5.6 million in a consent decree with Washington state for systematically denying pregnancy and nursing accommodations to its employees. The agreement, which follows a lawsuit alleging violations of state law and retaliation against workers, requires O’Reilly to compensate over 50 harmed employees and implement new policies and training. Although the company denies discrimination, it agreed to the settlement to focus on team member safety and wellness training.

feed.title: Is Coca-Cola's Digital Transformation a Catalyst for Margin Expansion?
feed.summary: The article discusses how Coca-Cola's digital transformation initiatives, including integrating digital tools across marketing, customer engagement, and supply-chain operations, are expected to drive efficiency and long-term margin expansion. It highlights similar efforts by peers like PepsiCo and Monster Beverage and notes that Coca-Cola's stock has outperformed its industry peers, with positive earnings growth projections. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Barco Joins The Coca-cola Company As Official Co-Presenting Sponsor Of CinemaCon 2026
feed.summary: Barco has announced its partnership with The Coca-Cola Company as an official co-presenting sponsor of CinemaCon 2026, marking the first time the convention will have two co-presenting sponsors since rebranding in 2011. This expanded partnership highlights Barco's commitment to the global theatrical experience, with the company also sponsoring a new Trade Show Networking Reception and showcasing its laser projection technology. CinemaCon, hosted by Cinema United, will take place from April 13-16 at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas.

feed.title: NYSE Owner Intercontinental Exchange Invests In OKX At $25B Valuation, Token Spikes 50%
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE), owner of the NYSE, has invested in crypto exchange OKX, valuing it at $25 billion and securing a board seat. This investment caused OKX's native token, OKB, to surge by approximately 50% initially. This move allows OKX users access to ICE's U.S. futures and tokenized equities markets, and ICE will use OKX's spot crypto prices to launch regulated crypto futures, marking ICE's third major crypto-related strategic move in six months.

feed.title: Expedia Partners with PredictHQ to Leverage Surging Sports-Tourism Demand
feed.summary: Expedia's stock is up 11.06% following its partnership with PredictHQ to capitalize on sports-tourism demand for the 2026 global soccer tournament, expecting over $8.1 billion in traveler spending. The company reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, surpassing expectations with increased revenue and EPS, while analysts have revised price targets favorably. Management's commitment to shareholder returns through repurchases and dividends further enhances investor confidence.

feed.title: Dollar General slides 4.5% as traders de-risk ahead of earnings and technicals cool off
feed.summary: Dollar General (DG) stock dropped 4.5% as traders de-risk before its upcoming quarterly earnings release on March 12, 2026. Technical indicators had shown the stock in "overbought" conditions, contributing to the profit-taking. Insider trading data reveals significant sales, while institutional investors show mixed activity ahead of the earnings call.

feed.title: Axon Gains From Strength in Connected Devices Unit: Can the Momentum Sustain?
feed.summary: Axon Enterprise, Inc. (AXON) is experiencing strong growth in its Connected Devices segment, driven by the popularity of its TASER 10 products, Axon Body 4 body-worn camera, and other solutions like counter-drone equipment and virtual reality training. In 2025, the segment's revenues increased by 29.1% to $1.58 billion. Despite this strong performance, Axon's stock valuation is high compared to its industry, trading at a forward price-to-earnings ratio of 70.34X versus the industry average of 48.94X, leading to a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Is Axon Enterprise, Inc. (AXON) A Good Stock To Buy?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Axon Enterprise, Inc. (AXON), a dominant force in public safety technology. Despite a temporary Q3 2025 EPS decline, the company demonstrates strong revenue growth, high gross margins, and strategic reinvestment in AI-driven software, positioning it for long-term success. Analysts largely maintain a bullish outlook, with average price targets above $750, indicating potential for significant upside.

feed.title: Hewlett-Packard announces it will leave Boise by the end of 2027
feed.summary: Hewlett-Packard (HP) has announced its plans to leave Boise by the end of 2027 as part of a larger global location strategy. The company intends to consolidate its Pacific Northwest operations around two hubs in Portland and Corvallis, Oregon. This move aims to reorganize HP around eight core sites prioritized for hiring and investment, and employees will be updated on relocation or telework options by May 1.

feed.title: Is Coca-Cola's Digital Transformation a Catalyst for Margin Expansion?
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is leveraging digital transformation across its global system to improve efficiency and profitability, leading to long-term margin expansion. This includes digitizing marketing, customer engagement, and supply-chain operations for better targeting and demand forecasting. Peers like PepsiCo and Monster Beverage are also adopting similar digital strategies to boost their own efficiencies and profitability.
